proof_trace: q6asm_audio_client_release (the kref callback invoked by q6asm_audio_client_free) only calls kfree(ac) and never frees ac->port[0].buf or ac->port[1].buf. These audio_buffer arrays are allocated by q6asm_map_memory_regions (via kzalloc at L516) and are normally freed by q6asm_unmap_memory_regions → q6asm_audio_client_free_buf, but q6asm_unmap_memory_regions skips q6asm_audio_client_free_buf when __q6asm_memory_unmap fails (L407-411 goto err path). Callers (q6asm_dai_close L454-456, q6asm_dai_compr_free L683-685) ignore the unmap return value and call q6asm_audio_client_free unconditionally, leaving port->buf leaked.
attack_scenario: An attacker with access to the PCM audio device triggers q6asm_map_memory_regions (allocating port->buf), then forces an APR send failure during q6asm_unmap_memory_regions (e.g., via DSP error or device stress), causing q6asm_audio_client_free_buf to be skipped; q6asm_audio_client_free then runs and kfree(ac) leaks the port buffer. Repeated prepare/close cycles under this error condition exhaust kernel memory.
reason: q6asm_unmap_memory_regions()で__q6asm_memory_unmap()がDSPエラーで失敗した場合、goto errでq6asm_audio_client_free_buf()がスキップされる。その後q6asm_audio_client_free()→kfree(ac)でport->bufがリーク。コード実確認済み。最新でも未修正。GT外の新発見。

proof_trace: q6apm_graph_open() at L257 allocates a graph resource that requires an explicit q6apm_graph_close() to release (as confirmed by q6apm_dai_close). On the four error paths after the graph is successfully opened (L271-274, L279-282, L286-289, L291-295), the single 'err:' label at L305 only calls kfree(prtd) and never calls q6apm_graph_close(prtd->graph), leaking the graph object. runtime->private_data is set only at L297 (after all constraints), so the ALSA close callback cannot recover the leaked resource.
attack_scenario: A process with access to the ALSA audio device opens a stream such that q6apm_graph_open succeeds but a subsequent snd_pcm_hw_constraint_* call fails (e.g., under memory pressure); repeated open attempts produce unbounded kernel graph resource accumulation.
reason: q6apm_dai_open()でq6apm_graph_open()成功後のエラーパスがq6apm_graph_close()を呼ばずkfree(prtd)のみ。グラフリソースリーク。最新でも未修正。GT外の新発見。

proof_trace: audioreach_alloc_graph_pkt() succeeds at line 66, storing a kzalloc'd gpr_pkt into graph->graph. When idr_alloc fails at line 75, the error path at lines 77-80 calls kfree(graph) to free the outer struct but never calls kfree(graph->graph), leaking the separately allocated packet buffer. kref_init (line 84) is only reached after idr_alloc succeeds, so the normal release path q6apm_put_audioreach_graph (which does call kfree(graph->graph) at line 141) is never invoked on this error path.
attack_scenario: A TOCTOU race allows two concurrent callers to both pass the idr_find check at line 45 (lock released between find and alloc), both allocate graph->graph, and then the second idr_alloc fails with -ENOSPC, leaking the gpr_pkt buffer; repeatedly triggering concurrent graph opens with the same graph_id causes unbounded kernel heap memory loss.
reason: GT RA-M4-O1一致。q6apm_get_audioreach_graph()でgraph->graphがidr_alloc失敗時に未解放。既知の旧バグ。

proof_trace: q6asm_audio_client_release() only calls kfree(ac) without freeing ac->port[n].buf, which is separately kzalloc'd in q6asm_map_memory_regions() (L516/L523). When q6asm_unmap_memory_regions() hits a DSP error (L407-410 goto err), it skips q6asm_audio_client_free_buf(), leaving port->buf live; q6asm_dai_compr_free() (L683-685) then unconditionally calls q6asm_audio_client_free(), so the kref release fires with non-NULL port->buf and leaks the allocation.
attack_scenario: A local user with /dev/snd/ access opens a compressed audio stream, triggers q6asm_map_memory_regions(), then closes the device while inducing a DSP unmap failure (timeout or firmware error at __q6asm_memory_unmap). Each open/map/close cycle leaks a kzalloc'd audio_buffer array; repeated cycles exhaust kernel memory, causing system denial of service.
reason: PROP-M4-mlk-001と同一根本原因の重複報告。q6asm_unmap_memory_regions()エラーパスでfree_bufスキップ→port->bufリーク。バグ自体は実在。最新でも未修正。
